name: yabasi

metadata.name: yabasi
metadata.summary: Minimal BASIC interpreter for running MININEC

documentation:
---
Author: Ralf Schlatterbeck \<<mailto:rsc@runtux.com>>
substitutions:
  --: |-
    ```{eval-rst}
    .. unicode:: U+2013   .. en dash
    ```
---

# Yabasi {{ -- }} Yet another BASIC Interpreter

This is a BASIC interpreter for an old dialect of the language used in
early IBM PCs {{ -- }} and, in fact even earlier incarnations of BASIC that
existed on a UNIVAC (according to [^footnote-1] the code there {{ -- }} which can be
run with Yabasi {{ -- }} ran on a UNIVAC).

It is written in Python. I wrote this over a weekend to
be able to run old [MININEC] code (the linked version of [MININEC]
contains some small fixes). I've later considerably enhanced it to also
support graphics, see below. It has almost no error checking (it
relies on the code being correct not trying to aid you in writing a new
program in BASIC, I think the world does not need new code in BASIC). If
you're looking for a working BASIC interpreter, look at the [pcbasic]
implementation, it is also in Python but faithfully reproduces the
memory limitations of the machines at the time. And it seems to use
single-precision floating point numbers. This is why I wrote my own
interpreter: I needed to compare computations in double precision and I
could not fit some examples into the limited memory of [pcbasic].

I'm probably not going to put much work into improving this code, it has
achieved the purpose: Running (and debugging in Python) old [MININEC]
code to allow me to compare the computations in BASIC to my
re-implementation of [MININEC] in Python, [pymininec].

But in fact the code can now run more than the publicly available
version of [MININEC] and implements some of the basic binary file I/O
mechanisms of the time.

## Graphics

The latest version can emulate text-based interfaces with cursor
movement and graphics using an emulated CGA high-resolution mode.
To turn on this feature, Yabasi needs to be called with the

```
--screen=tkinter
```

option (which can be abbreviated with `-S tkinter`). This emulates the
text mode of a CGA graphics card and the (what counted at the time as)
high-resolution (640x200) mode for graphics. The latter had
double-height lines and is emulated in tkinter with a 640x400 canvas.

The reason for this change is to be able to run the "GRAPS" graphics
package [^footnote-2] which was used by many technical reports of the time.
One of those reports is the MiniNec version 3 report [^footnote-3] which I'm
especially interested in.

Interesting today is that GRAPS (besides showing the plot on the
emulated CGA screen) can export graphics in the HPGL plotter language
which can be turned into graphics formats that can be used today.
On my Debian Linux I'm using the `hp2xx` package for this.

To make text in the graphics mode work, you need a special double-height
CGA font. I've successfully used the font `Mx437_IBM_CGA-2y.ttf` from
[The Ultimate Oldschool PC Font Pack]. You may still run into trouble
if your system does scale fonts differently. On my Linux X-Windows
system I had to scale the font with 12pt, this may be different on other
systems. See the line in Yabasi with the string

```
Mx437 IBM CGA-2y
```

this line has the scaling of the font in the second parameter. An
example of a GRAPS screen plot using this font is here:

:::{figure} https://raw.githubusercontent.com/schlatterbeck/yabasi/master/loglinh.png
:align: center
:::

Compare this figure to the original in the GRAPS report [^footnote-2] on page
D-10. Note that unfortunately the pages in the GRAPS report are mixed
up (my PDF view shows it as page 141), I've made a version with the
pages in the correct order if anybody is interested.

## Changes

Version 2.0: Support Text mode and CGA type-2 graphics using tkinter

This version now supports a grapic mode based on the tkinter toolkit
(coming with python). It supports a text interface (using a text screen)
and a graphical interface (emulating a CGA card in high resolution
monochrome mode).
The version needs a CGA font with double height.

- tkinter-based text and graphics
- lots of bug-fixes
- Considerable enhancement of the supported Basic primitives
- implement different graphics/text backends (currently two, the old
  default text-based interface and the tkinter backend)

Version 1.0: More fixes

This version has a lot of fixes that go beyond just running [MININEC], it
can now be used to read binary files ([MININEC] used a pre-processor
script to create a binary representation of an antenna geometry) and it
can run programs without a line number on every line.

- We no longer require line numbers on every line
- Handle multi-line `IF`/`END IF`
- Dynamic `DIM` statements
- Binary File I/O with `GET`/`PUT`, note that a file is opened for
  binary if a record length is specified, this probably should change at
  some time to just convert everything written to a file from python's
  string representation where necessary and open all files in binary
  mode. We use the default in Basic of opening a file read/write, so
  don't be surprised if a file opened for reading which doesn't exist is
  created with zero length.
- Conversion of int and float from/to strings {{ -- }} in python the strings
  are represented as bytes objects {{ -- }} these are the functions `MKI$`,
  `MKS$` and fixes to `CVI` and `CVS`
- Bug-fix when a `GOSUB` is not the last statement in a list of
  statements (e.g. in `IF` *expr* `THEN` *list-of-statements*), this
  did not execute the `GOSUB` previously

Version 0.3: Fix rules for printlist

The syntax of parameters to the `PRINT` statement can either use a
semicolon (or a comma) to separate expressions or just put the
expressions together without a separator. The latter had some quite
ad-hoc rules and I've removed many of them, the result removes a
reduce/reduce conflict...

Version 0.2: First working (released) version

[^footnote-1]: Alfredo J. Julian, James C. Logan, and John W. Rockway.
    Mininec: A mini-numerical electromagnetics code. Technical Report
    NOSC TD 516, Naval Ocean Systems Center (NOSC), San Diego,
    California, September 1982. Available as [ADA121535].

[^footnote-2]: R. T. Laird. GRAPS: GRAphical Plotting System. Technical
    Document 820, Naval Ocean Systems Center, July 1985. Available as
    [ADA159808].

[^footnote-3]: J. C. Logan and J. W. Rockway. The new MININEC (version 3):
    A mini-numerical electromagnetic code. Technical Report NOSC TD 938,
    Naval Ocean Systems Center (NOSC), San Diego, California, September
    1986\. Available (in very bad quality) as [ADA181682]. See also my
    [transcription on github].

[ada121535]: https://apps.dtic.mil/sti/pdfs/ADA121535.pdf
[ada159808]: https://apps.dtic.mil/sti/tr/pdf/ADA159808.pdf
[ada181682]: https://apps.dtic.mil/sti/pdfs/ADA181682.pdf
[mininec]: https://github.com/schlatterbeck/MiniNec
[pcbasic]: https://robhagemans.github.io/pcbasic/
[pymininec]: https://github.com/schlatterbeck/pymininec
[the ultimate oldschool pc font pack]: https://int10h.org/oldschool-pc-fonts/
[transcription on github]: https://github.com/schlatterbeck/mininec-3-doc/blob/master/mininec3.pdf

functions.name: yabasi.bas.fun_fractional_part
documentation:
This is used in the first Mininec implementation, probably
something that the UNIVAC BASIC at the time provided.
I could not find it in any BASIC function references.

classes.name: yabasi.bas.Screen

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Screen.cmd_key
documentation: Since we cannot get function key input do nothing

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Screen.cmd_locate
documentation: Positions cursor, we just go to start of line

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Screen.fun_inkey
documentation:
In the programs we support with the simple screen, INKEY$ is
used for clearing the input buffer, no need to do this with,
e.g., cooked terminal mode on Linux. So we always return an
empty string.
Default screen emulation doing essentially nothing

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Screen_Tkinter.dump_contents
documentation:
Dump contents of text or graphics screen (depending on
current graphics mode) to the test object

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Screen_Tkinter.get_canvas_rectangle
documentation:
This is a hack: It screen-grabs the rectangle. So if the
canvas is obscured by another window this will yield very
interesting special effects.
There seems to be only a postscript export of the tkinter
canvas (and this exports the whole thing, meaning it's slow:
It *does* have a view area (x, y, width, height) but still
needs to render the whole thing and then extract the area)
This relies on the canvas having a border of 1, otherwise we
would need to take it into account
This returns a boolean numpy array.

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Screen_Tkinter.cmd_color
documentation:
Currently only support colors in text mode
We ignore the third argument (border) if given.
This would need changes if we allow empty parameters
(i.e. a comma without an expression before the comma)

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Screen_Tkinter.cmd_get_graphics
documentation:
This currently works only for the graphics mode 2
with 1 bit per pixel
Since the result can be ambiguous (the two lines in the
canvas representing one Basic line may not have the same
content) we or the K rows/cols representing one Basic line.

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Screen_Tkinter.cmd_key
documentation: Set macro for function key given with first expression

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Screen_Tkinter.cmd_print
documentation:
tk row is 1-based, col is 0-based
Basic row/col is 1-based
we compute an index into our buffer

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Screen_Tkinter.cmd_print_canvas
documentation: We generally asume non-multiline strings and no text-wrapping

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Screen_Tkinter.cmd_pset
documentation:
Only the variant without attribute is implemented.
Has the effect of changing the current graphics position.

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Screen_Tkinter.cmd_put_graphics
documentation: For now this only works with 1 bit per pixel

classes.name: yabasi.bas.Print_Using
documentation:
Formatter for USING in a print statement
\>>> p = Print_Using ('###.##    ')
\>>> p.fmt
\['%6.2f    '\]
\>>> p = Print_Using ('       ##.###^^^^')
\>>> p.fmt
\['       %.4E'\]
\>>> p = Print_Using ('   ###.##   ')
\>>> p.fmt
\['   %6.2f   '\]
\>>> p = Print_Using ('   ###.##')
\>>> p.fmt
\['   %6.2f'\]
\>>> p = Print_Using ('###  ###   ##')
\>>> p.fmt
\['%3.0f  ', '%3.0f   ', '%2.0f'\]

classes.name: yabasi.bas.Exec_Stack
documentation: Stack holding multiline IF/ELSE and FOR/NEXT info

classes.name: yabasi.bas.Context
documentation:
Context of current execution, include line numbers to be able to
restore execution after a GOSUB.

classes.name: yabasi.bas.Stack_Entry
documentation:
We stack FOR/NEXT and IF/ELSE
While a condition is False we just skip commands but we still
*do* pick up FOR loops and multi-line IF/ELSE

classes.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter_Test
documentation:
This is used for testing: redirecting output, optionally
redirecting input and passing the program as an iterable.
In addition we can capture the tkinter text or graphics
screen.

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.fun_eof
documentation: FUNCTIONS which need access to interpreter

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.fun_fn
documentation:
Temporarily bind function args to values from exprlist then
call the function, then restore args.

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.cmd_close
documentation:
Seems a missing file handle closes all files
We interpret a missing '#' as the same file as with '#'

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.cmd_defsng
documentation: We ignore single precision setting, all is double

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.cmd_end
documentation:
We treat the 'SYSTEM' command same as 'END'
In some Basic variants the interpreter stops on END and
waits for something to completely terminate, we don't do
this.

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.cmd_keyoff
documentation:
Command 'KEY OFF'
This is supposed to turn off a list of function-key macros
at the bottom of the screen. We do nothing.

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.cmd_lset
documentation:
According to pcbasic docs lset will do nothing if the
variable is not defined. But apparently some mininec
implementations use this with variables undefined. It may be
a bug in the mininec implementation but we allow undefined
variables. In case of undefined variables we treat this like
an assignment.

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.cmd_rset
documentation:
According to pcbasic docs lset will do nothing if the
variable is not defined. But apparently some mininec
implementations use LSET with variables undefined. It may be
a bug in the mininec implementation but we allow undefined
variables. In case of undefined variables we treat this like
an assignment. We make RSET mirror the behavior of LSET.

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_stmt
documentation:
simple-statement : assignment-statement
                 | call-statement
                 | circle-statement
                 | close-statement
                 | cls-statement
                 | color-statement
                 | data-statement
                 | def-statement
                 | defint-statement
                 | defsng-statement
                 | dim-statement
                 | else-statement
                 | endif-statement
                 | end-statement
                 | error-statement
                 | field-statement
                 | for-statement
                 | get-statement
                 | gosub-statement
                 | goto-statement
                 | if-start-statement
                 | if-statement
                 | input-statement
                 | key-statement
                 | kill-statement
                 | line-statement
                 | line-input-statement
                 | locate-statement
                 | lset-statement
                 | mid-statement
                 | next-statement
                 | onerrgoto-statement
                 | ongosub-statement
                 | ongoto-statement
                 | open-statement
                 | print-statement
                 | pset-statement
                 | put-statement
                 | read-statement
                 | rem-statement
                 | reset-statement
                 | restore-statement
                 | resume-statement
                 | return-statement
                 | rset-statement
                 | shell-statement
                 | screen-statement
                 | wend-statement
                 | while-statement
                 | width-statement
                 | window-statement
                 | write-statement

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_circle_opt
documentation:
circle-opt :
           | COMMA opt-expr
           | COMMA opt-expr COMMA opt-expr
           | COMMA opt-expr COMMA opt-expr COMMA opt-expr
           | COMMA opt-expr COMMA opt-expr COMMA opt-expr COMMA expr

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_circle_statement
documentation: circle-statement : CIRCLE coord COMMA expr circle-opt

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_close_statement
documentation:
close-statement : CLOSE
                | CLOSE FHANDLE
                | CLOSE NUMBER

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_coord
documentation: coord : LPAREN expr COMMA expr RPAREN

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_def_statement
documentation:
def-statement : DEF FNFUNCTION LPAREN varlist RPAREN EQ expr
              | DEF VAR VAR LPAREN varlist RPAREN EQ expr

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_expression_function
documentation:
expr : ABS   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | ASC   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | ATN   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | CHR   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | COS   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | CVI   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | CVS   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | EOF   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | FIX   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | FRP   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | INT   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | LEN   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | LOG   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | MKI   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | MKS   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | SGN   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | SIN   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | SPACE LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | SQR   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | STR   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | TAB   LPAREN expr RPAREN
     | VAL   LPAREN expr RPAREN

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_expression_function_2
documentation:
expr : LEFT   LPAREN expr COMMA expr RPAREN
     | RIGHT  LPAREN expr COMMA expr RPAREN
     | STRING LPAREN expr COMMA expr RPAREN

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_expression_function_2_3
documentation:
expr : MID   LPAREN expr COMMA expr COMMA expr RPAREN
     | MID   LPAREN expr COMMA expr RPAREN
     | INSTR LPAREN expr COMMA expr RPAREN
     | INSTR LPAREN expr COMMA expr COMMA expr RPAREN

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_expression_twoop
documentation:
expr : expr PLUS   expr
     | expr MINUS  expr
     | expr TIMES  expr
     | expr DIVIDE expr
     | expr MOD    expr
     | expr GT     expr
     | expr GE     expr
     | expr LT     expr
     | expr LE     expr
     | expr NE     expr
     | expr EQ     expr
     | expr AND    expr
     | expr OR     expr
     | expr EXPO   expr
     | expr INTDIV expr

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_fieldlist
documentation:
fieldlist : NUMBER AS lhs
          | fieldlist COMMA NUMBER AS lhs

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_for_statement
documentation: for-statement : FOR VAR EQ expr TO expr

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_for_statement_step
documentation: for-statement : FOR VAR EQ expr TO expr STEP expr

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_get_statement
documentation:
get-statement : GET FHANDLE
              | GET NUMBER
              | GET coord MINUS coord COMMA VAR

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_if_statement
documentation:
if-statement : IF expr THEN NUMBER
             | IF expr THEN NUMBER ELSE NUMBER
             | IF expr THEN NUMBER ELSE statement
             | IF expr THEN statement
             | IF expr THEN statement ELSE NUMBER
             | IF expr THEN statement ELSE statement

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_input_statement
documentation:
input-statement : INPUT STRING_SQ SEMIC varlist-complex
                | INPUT STRING_DQ SEMIC varlist-complex
                | INPUT SEMIC varlist-complex

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_input_statement_multi
documentation:
input-statement : INPUT varlist-complex
                | INPUT FHANDLE COMMA varlist-complex

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_intlist
documentation:
intlist : NUMBER
        | HEXNUMBER
        | intlist COMMA NUMBER
        | intlist COMMA HEXNUMBER

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_key_statement
documentation:
key-statement : KEY expr COMMA expr
              | KEY VAR

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_lhs
documentation:
lhs : VAR
    | VAR LPAREN exprlist RPAREN

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_line_opt
documentation:
line-opt :
         | COMMA line-attr
         | COMMA line-attr COMMA line-bf
         | COMMA line-attr COMMA line-bf COMMA expr

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_line_statement
documentation: line-statement : LINE coord-opt MINUS coord line-opt

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_line_input_statement
documentation:
line-input-statement : LINE INPUT FHANDLE COMMA lhs
                     | LINE INPUT SEMIC lhs

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_locate_statement
documentation:
locate-statement : LOCATE opt-expr COMMA opt-expr
                 | LOCATE opt-expr COMMA opt-expr COMMA exprlist

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_mid_statement
documentation:
mid-statement : MID LPAREN lhs COMMA expr COMMA expr RPAREN EQ expr
              | MID LPAREN lhs COMMA expr RPAREN EQ expr

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_open_statement
documentation:
open-statement : OPEN expr FOR OUTPUT AS FHANDLE
               | OPEN expr FOR APPEND AS FHANDLE
               | OPEN expr FOR INPUT  AS FHANDLE
               | OPEN expr FOR RANDOM AS FHANDLE

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_open_statement_bin
documentation:
open-statement : OPEN expr AS FHANDLE LEN EQ expr
               | OPEN expr AS FHANDLE
               | OPEN expr FOR RANDOM AS FHANDLE LEN EQ expr
               | OPEN expr FOR OUTPUT AS FHANDLE LEN EQ expr
               | OPEN expr FOR INPUT  AS FHANDLE LEN EQ expr

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_print_statement
documentation:
print-statement : PRINT printlist
                | PRINT USING printlist
                | PRINT FHANDLE COMMA printlist
                | PRINT FHANDLE COMMA USING printlist
                | QMARK printlist
                | QMARK USING printlist
                | QMARK FHANDLE COMMA printlist
                | QMARK FHANDLE COMMA USING printlist

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_printlist
documentation:
printlist : empty
          | expr
          | printlist SEMIC expr
          | printlist COMMA expr
          | printlist expr
          | printlist SEMIC
          | printlist COMMA

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_put_option
documentation:
put-option :
           | COMMA PSET
           | COMMA PRESET
           | COMMA AND
           | COMMA OR
           | COMMA XOR

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_put_statement
documentation:
put-statement : PUT FHANDLE
              | PUT NUMBER
              | PUT FHANDLE COMMA NUMBER
              | PUT NUMBER  COMMA NUMBER
              | PUT coord COMMA VAR put-option

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_resume_statement
documentation:
resume-statement : RESUME NUMBER
                 | RESUME
                 | RESUME NEXT

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_screen_statement
documentation: screen-statement : SCREEN expr COMMA expr COMMA expr COMMA expr

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_varlist
documentation:
varlist : varlist COMMA VAR
        | varlist MINUS VAR
        | VAR

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_width_statement
documentation:
width-statement : WIDTH expr
                | WIDTH expr COMMA expr

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_window_statement
documentation:
window-statement : WINDOW
                 | WINDOW coord MINUS coord
                 | WINDOW SCREEN coord MINUS coord

methods.name: yabasi.bas.Interpreter.p_write
documentation:
write-statement : WRITE FHANDLE COMMA exprlist
                | WRITE exprlist
